summary: preinstall script beacons host information to hardcoded third-party domain
details: The package's dist/scripts/preinstall.js runs at install time (npm preinstall lifecycle) and contacts a hardcoded external host at https://2tak.l.serverhost.name using https.get (lines 78-80 and 192-194). The script also pulls host identifiers via os.userInfo() (line 7) and os.networkInterfaces() (line 227), and reads process.env (line 17), alongside filesystem probes (fs.existsSync on lines 23 and 52). The combination of install-time execution, hardcoded non-publisher C2 domain, host/user/network enumeration, and environment variable access matches the active-attack / install-time exfiltration shape: any developer or CI system running `npm install @gusmano/reext` will transmit machine identity data to an attacker-controlled host without consent. The domain serverhost.name is a generic dynamic-DNS / hosting provider, not a vendor-owned CDN matching the package's scope. No legitimate install-time purpose requires beaconing host identity to an unrelated third-party domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
